elements.content:
Prompt Enters the System

Your message leaves the app and hits the front door of the Claude-style backend: the API gateway.

- Client app sends the request
- Gateway receives the payload
- Routing starts here
Prompt Enters the System

The Gateway Does Bouncer Work

Before the request goes deeper, the gateway checks identity, limits abuse, and sends traffic to the right place.

- Validate auth token
- Apply rate limits
- Route to orchestration
The Gateway Does Bouncer Work

Safety Runs Early

The prompt gets scanned before expensive model work begins, so risky requests can be blocked or handled safely.

- Policy signals are detected
- Unsafe patterns get flagged
- Approved prompts continue
Safety Runs Early

Context Gets Assembled

The model does not see only your latest sentence. It gets a packaged prompt with instructions and recent conversation state.

- Your message
- System instructions
- Recent chat history
- Relevant context bundle
Context Gets Assembled

The Model Starts Thinking

The packaged prompt is broken into tokens, pushed through model servers, and accelerated on GPUs.

- Text becomes tokens
- Tokens enter the model
- GPUs run the math
- New tokens are predicted
The Model Starts Thinking

Tokens Stream Back

The answer usually returns piece by piece, which is why text can appear before the whole response is finished.

- Model emits token chunks
- Pipeline relays them back
- Client renders live text
Tokens Stream Back

Output Gets Re-Checked

Before the response leaves, a second safety pass reviews the generated text for last-mile issues.

- Generated answer is scanned
- Edge cases can be caught
- Safe output continues
Output Gets Re-Checked

The Answer Gets Packaged

The formatter turns raw generated text into the clean response you see, including structure like lists, code blocks, or citations.

- Apply message formatting
- Add code blocks if needed
- Attach citations when available
The Answer Gets Packaged

Response Delivered

The answer reaches the app while telemetry watches system health in the background.

- User receives the response
- Latency is measured
- Throughput is tracked
- Errors are logged
Response Delivered
